data.description:
# Headline
Stabilize proxy session-cache routing and streaming safeguards to prevent full-prompt regressions and runaway repetitive outputs under multi-turn load.

## Problem statement
Proxy behavior still regresses between efficient delta routing and costly full-prompt resend, and can allow long repetitive generations before cancellation. This creates high CPU usage, unstable latency, and poor response quality.

## Users
- **Primary users:** operators and developers running llama-server through the proxy.
- **User stories:**
  - As an operator, I want stable incremental routing so follow-up turns do not repeatedly replay full conversation history.
  - As a developer, I want proxy-level safeguards to stop pathological long repetitive generations before they consume excessive resources.
  - As an on-call engineer, I want observability that explains why fallbacks happened so regressions are diagnosable quickly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Per-session single-flight protection is implemented so only one in-flight generation is allowed per session, with queue/reject behavior explicitly implemented and validated by tests.
- Proxy enforces configurable completion-length and stream-runtime guardrails with safe defaults and documented overrides.
- A repetition/loop safety mechanism stops pathological repeated output and marks the session for configured cleanup/invalidation handling.
- Automated tests verify:
  - healthy continuation does not regress into full-prompt resend,
  - same-session concurrency handling,
  - runaway/repetition cutoff behavior,
  - session history integrity after canceled streams.
- Structured observability reports:
  - full-prompt fallback reasons,
  - per-session concurrency queue/reject events,
  - loop-cutoff events,
  - forced session invalidation events.
- Representative runtime evidence (test logs or reproducible local run logs) shows no repeated text loop and no persistent full-prompt resend regression.
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

## Constraints
- Changes must be proxy-side first and must not depend on llama-server core modifications.
- Existing session behavior and compatibility headers must remain backward compatible for current clients.
- Guardrails must be configurable so operators can tune limits without code changes.
- Intake scope is stabilization of current behavior; additional enhancements discovered during implementation must be tracked as separate linked work items rather than added to this scope.

## Existing state
- Prior work fixed part of restore signaling and demonstrated token-size reduction in some continuations.
- Logs still show cases of full prompt re-processing and at least one runaway generation event with very high generated token count before cancellation.
- Current work item already captures RCA evidence linking fallback reasons, cache behavior, and loop symptoms.

## Desired change
Introduce a cohesive proxy stabilization package that makes delta routing behavior predictable, adds strong anti-runaway guardrails, preserves session-history correctness, and emits reliable diagnostics for fallback and mitigation events.

## Related work
### Potentially related docs
- `proxy/README.md` — documents current session-based incremental ingestion, strict restore policy, and session observability endpoints.
- `proxy/proxy/server.py` — primary request path where delta routing and stream handling are implemented.
- `proxy/tests/test_incremental_ingestion.py` — existing regression tests around session delta routing and restore fallback logic.
- `LLAMA_README.md` — high-level integration notes for llama-server and session-based ingestion behavior.

### Potentially related work items
- Investigate high CPU usage in llama-server (LP-0MPNTJ165004M5GX) — root-cause analysis parent describing CPU impact and follow-up chain.
- Stop full prompt resend caused by missing_restore_signal (LP-0MPNTTH7X000W386) — prior bug fix for strict restore gating and fallback behavior.
- Verify context cache behavior from live proxy logs (LP-0MPNVNL0W008Z0L4) — runtime validation showing partial recovery and remaining cache inconsistencies.
- Implement incremental session-based prompt ingestion for llama-server (LP-0MO6DJP0P006WCHA) — earlier feature baseline for session IDs, delta ingestion, and observability patterns.

### Related work (automated report)
- `proxy/README.md` details strict restore behavior, fallback reasons, and session metrics, making it the primary operational reference for expected proxy behavior before and after this fix.
- `proxy/proxy/server.py` contains the delta-routing decision point (`_classify_delta_routing`) and stream handling path where single-flight control, loop cutoffs, and guardrails must be implemented.
- `proxy/tests/test_incremental_ingestion.py` already validates restore/delta edge cases and is the best baseline to extend for non-regression and cancellation-history integrity tests.
- Stop full prompt resend caused by missing_restore_signal (LP-0MPNTTH7X000W386) implemented earlier restore-gating fixes; this item extends that work to cover concurrency and runaway output safeguards.
- Verify context cache behavior from live proxy logs (LP-0MPNVNL0W008Z0L4) provides concrete evidence that delta forwarding can work while showing remaining restore inconsistencies.
- Investigate high CPU usage in llama-server (LP-0MPNTJ165004M5GX) establishes the production impact context and links CPU stress to prompt replay behavior.

## Risks & assumptions
- **Risk:** Guardrail thresholds that are too strict may truncate valid long responses. **Mitigation (note):** keep limits configurable and validated by targeted tests.
- **Risk:** Same-session serialization may increase perceived latency for overlapping user requests. **Mitigation (note):** expose queue/reject events in metrics for tuning.
- **Risk:** Scope creep into broader llama-server internals. **Mitigation (required):** capture extra enhancements/refactors as separate linked work items.
- **Assumption:** Proxy has enough control in request/stream lifecycle to enforce cutoff and invalidation behavior without upstream API changes.
- **Assumption:** Existing observability infrastructure can be extended with new counters/events required by this item.

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- No clarifying questions were asked in this intake run. Answer source: existing work item description and related work-item evidence. Final: yes.
data.title: Proxy RCA and full stabilization for cache path and runaway loop responses

## Summary
Rebuild the llama.cpp binary used by the proxy so that the /slots/<id>/save and /slots/<id>/restore endpoints are available. Update documentation to make the build reproducible.

## Problem statement
The current llama-server build returns 404 for /slots/<id>/save even when --slot-save-path is set, preventing slot persistence and causing context loss.

## User story
- As an operator, I want slot save/restore endpoints to work so the proxy can persist KV cache state between requests.

## Expected behavior
- llama-server responds 200 to POST /slots/<id>/save and /slots/<id>/restore when --slot-save-path is configured.
- The build steps are documented so the binary can be reproduced in the llama distrobox container.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A reproducible build procedure is documented (README or docs) including repo version/commit and build flags.
- The rebuilt llama-server binary is used by the proxy (or documented path for use).
- Manual verification evidence: /slots/<id>/save and /slots/<id>/restore return 200 when called with a valid filename.
- Any required config changes or environment variables are documented.

## Notes
- Context: slot persistence required for proxy cache stability.
data.title: Rebuild llama.cpp with slot save/restore endpoints

data.description:
## Summary
Ensure KV cache persistence works in router mode by sending the model name with slot save/restore requests and using the resolved llama model id for slot availability checks.

## Problem statement
In router mode, llama-server returns 400 for /slots/{id}?action=save|restore unless a model name is included in the request body. The proxy currently omits this field, so slot save/restore never succeeds and KV cache is not reused.

## User story
- As an operator, I want slot save/restore calls from the proxy to succeed in router mode so session KV cache persists across requests.

## Expected behavior
- Proxy includes the resolved llama model name in slot save/restore requests when router mode is enabled.
- Slot availability checks use the resolved llama model id, avoiding 400 errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover model inclusion in slot save/restore requests and model resolution behavior.
- Proxy sends slot save/restore requests with model name when router mode is enabled.
- Slot availability checks query /slots with the resolved llama model id.
- Manual verification notes confirm slot save/restore succeeds (200) and logs show slot_save/slot_restore success.
data.title: Include router model in slot save/restore requests

## Summary
Add explicit proxy logging that records whether a session id header was provided on incoming chat requests and which header was used.

## User Story
As an operator, I want to confirm in proxy logs whether client requests are sending session identifiers so I can diagnose context-loss issues quickly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- When a chat request is processed, the proxy logs which session header (`X-Session-Id`, `session_id`, `X-Client-Request-Id`, `X-Session-Affinity`) was used, including a truncated session id value.
- When no session header is provided, the proxy logs a clear message indicating a new session id will be generated.
- Log messages avoid full session id disclosure (truncate to prefix) and use the existing llama-proxy logger.
- Unit tests cover logging for both header-present and header-missing cases.

## Deliverables
- Code updates in `proxy/proxy/server.py`.
- Tests in `proxy/tests/test_incremental_ingestion.py`.

data.description:
Refactor proxy server — Intake draft (LP-0MPQ1VMX0000GNF4)

Headline
--------
Refactor the monolithic proxy implementation (proxy/proxy/server.py) into several smaller, well-scoped modules to improve maintainability, testability, and reduce regression risk. Scope: Medium — split into 4–6 modules, add/extend unit and integration tests for key subsystems, and update documentation. Work to be delivered as incremental PRs.

Problem statement
-----------------
The proxy server implementation has become large and hard to navigate (the primary runtime is concentrated in proxy/proxy/server.py). This increases cognitive overhead for contributors, raises the risk of regressions when making changes, and makes targeted testing and ownership harder.

Users
-----
- Backend maintainers and developers who need to read and change proxy behavior.
- SREs and operators who rely on stable runtime semantics (health, metrics, model-loading behavior).
- QA and automation engineers who need clear test boundaries and reliable unit/integration tests.

Example user stories
- As a developer, I want the proxy code organized into small modules so I can find and change behavior in one area without understanding unrelated code.
- As an SRE, I want refactors to preserve health/metrics behavior so operator tooling and runbooks remain valid.
- As a QA engineer, I want focused unit tests for model-loading, session handling, and routing so regressions are caught early.

Acceptance Criteria (Success Criteria)
-------------------------------------
1. Full test validation: The full project test suite (unit + integration tests) must pass locally before changes are committed and after merge. (Measurable: pytest returns zero failures for all test runs used by CI.)

2. Code modularization: proxy/proxy/server.py is split into 4–6 smaller modules (suggested modules: routing/handlers, model lifecycle & loader, session management, metrics/observability, utils/helpers). No resulting module should exceed ~1000 lines of code (measured by `wc -l`).

3. Behavioral parity tests: Each extracted subsystem (model loading, session handling, health/readiness, request routing) must keep or add unit tests that assert existing observable behavior (including model-loading 503 semantics, session delta routing, and concurrency/slot handling).

4. Documentation: All related documentation is updated to reflect the new code layout and any subtle implementation details changed during refactor. This includes code comments, proxy/README.md, and any linked runbooks. (Measurable: changed docs are included in the PR and noted in PR description.)

5. Integration validation: At least one end-to-end/integration test covering model switch and session-based incremental ingestion must remain passing (or be updated) to demonstrate no regressions in critical paths.

Notes: If any acceptance criterion is unclear in scope (for example the exact module boundaries or line limit), see Appendix clarifying questions.

Constraints
-----------
- Delivery style: Prefer incremental PRs (small, reviewable changes) rather than a single large PR. Keep each PR self-contained and runnable.
- Public API changes: Allowed but should be minimized. Any breaking changes must include a migration note and tests demonstrating the migration path.
- Preserve operator-visible semantics: health endpoint, model-loading 503+retry behavior, slot/concurrency signals, and metrics must remain observable and compatible with existing runbooks unless explicitly changed and documented.
- Keep changes local to proxy/ where possible; avoid cross-cutting global rewrites in unrelated packages during the same work item.

Existing state
--------------
- The main proxy runtime is implemented in proxy/proxy/server.py (monolithic, several thousand lines). It currently implements routing, session management, model lifecycle/start-stop, metrics, web UI, and admin endpoints.
- Tests: proxy/tests/ contains unit and integration tests for session ingestion, backend resilience, model lifecycle, and logs. Some tests rely on the current shape of server internals (may need patching to follow refactor boundaries).
- Docs: proxy/README.md and docs/llama-router.md document key runtime behaviors (session semantics, model-loading 503 + retry_after behavior, router-mode notes) and must be updated if internal behavior is changed in any way.
- Related work: several existing work items touch the same code paths (see Related work below); some are completed and contain precedent tests and design notes.

Desired change (implementation notes)
-------------------------------------
- Identify logical boundaries in proxy/proxy/server.py and extract modules. Suggested minimal split:
  - proxy/handlers.py (FastAPI route handlers / HTTP glue)
  - proxy/lifecycle.py (model load/unload, background loaders, model_switch_refcount)
  - proxy/session.py (SessionManager interactions, delta calculation, single-flight coordination)
  - proxy/observability.py (metrics, health endpoint, SSE status)
  - proxy/utils.py (helpers, config parsing, HTTP client wrapper)

- For each extracted module:
  - Move code incrementally, keeping public function signatures or providing thin adapters/tests until callers are updated.
  - Add or move unit tests into proxy/tests/ to cover behavior at module boundaries.
  - Run the full test suite locally between PRs; fix regressions as part of the PR.

- PR strategy: create a small sequence of incremental PRs, for example:
  1. Extract one subsystem (e.g., handlers -> proxy/handlers.py) + tests and docs update.
  2. Extract lifecycle -> proxy/lifecycle.py, add adapters and tests.
  3. Extract session management -> proxy/session.py and ensure session tests pass.
  4. Consolidate observability/metrics and finalize cleanup.

- Validation: each PR must run `pytest` in the repo root (or equivalent CI target) and include updated or new tests demonstrating parity.

Related work
------------
Potentially related work items (selected):
- Return informative 503 with Retry-After and machine-readable body when no slots available — LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ — slot-exhaustion behavior and tests (intake_complete). Refactor must preserve or update this behavior and its tests.
- Mitigate llama-server ROCm crash path and add backend recovery safeguards — LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 — concurrency gating, retry/backoff, watchdog and health semantics (in-review); related for crash-path behavior.
- Implement incremental session-based prompt ingestion for llama-server — LP-0MO6DJP0P006WCHA — session/delta ingestion (completed); refactor must preserve these features and tests.
- Add session header resolution logging — LP-0MPOOT5P3007CZLF — recent small changes to logging and tests around session header resolution.
- Investigate proxy not responding — LP-0MNTBX862007HC2V — incident investigation; relevant for regression risk on request routing.

Potentially related docs & files:
- proxy/README.md — operator and developer-facing documentation describing health, session semantics, and model-loading behavior.
- docs/llama-router.md — router-mode notes and model lifecycle considerations.
- proxy/tests/ (test files noted above) — use as references and carry-forward tests.

Risks & assumptions
-------------------
- Risk: Regressions in critical paths (model-loading, session delta routing, health/readiness). Mitigation: add/extend unit and integration tests, run full test suite before merge, and use incremental PRs so changes are reviewable.
- Risk: Operator or monitoring tooling breaks if observable metrics or health semantics change. Mitigation: preserve existing metrics names and /health semantics unless explicitly changed and documented; include migration notes for any deliberate change.
- Risk: Tests that rely on internal structure may be brittle during refactor. Mitigation: preserve public function signatures with thin adapters while tests are updated; prefer black-box tests for key behaviors.
- Assumption: Developers can run the full test suite locally and CI enforces the same checks.
- Scope-control: Any additional features or API redesign discovered during the refactor should be recorded as separate linked work items rather than expanded into this work item.

Related work (automated report)
-------------------------------
- LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ — "Return informative 503 with Retry-After and machine-readable body when no slots available" (intake_complete). Relevant: contains slot-exhaustion behavior and unit/integration tests the refactor must preserve or adapt.
- LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 — "Mitigate llama-server ROCm crash path and add backend recovery safeguards" (in-review). Relevant: concurrency gating, retry/backoff, and health/readiness semantics that must remain observable.
- LP-0MO6DJP0P006WCHA — "Implement incremental session-based prompt ingestion for llama-server" (completed). Relevant: session/delta ingestion behaviors and tests that must be preserved.
- LP-0MPOOT5P3007CZLF — "Add session header resolution logging" (completed). Relevant: small logging & test changes around session header handling.
- LP-0MNTBX862007HC2V — "Investigate proxy not responding" (completed). Relevant: incident context and regression risks for request routing.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
---------------------------------------
- Q: "1) Scope of the refactor — pick one:
   - A) Small: split proxy/proxy/server.py into logical modules (routes, model lifecycle/load, session, metrics, utils) with no behavior changes; add missing unit tests where needed.
   - B) Medium: A + improve test coverage, add targeted unit/integration tests, and tidy internal APIs (minor internal signature changes allowed with tests).
   - C) Large: redesign internals and public APIs (requires migration plan, multiple dependent work items).
   (Suggested default if you don’t care: B)"
  — Answer (user): "B". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "2) Acceptance criteria (pick from this checklist; you may pick multiple or provide custom items):
   - 1) Full project test suite passes with no behavioral regressions.
   - 2) No user-visible API changes (backwards compatible).
   - 3) server.py is split into N smaller modules (I can suggest N=4–6) and no module is >~1000 lines.
   - 4) Key subsystems (model loading, session handling, health/metrics, request routing) have unit tests asserting same behavior as before.
   - 5) All related documentation and code comments are updated where relevant.
   (Please confirm which of the above are required and add any measurable targets, e.g., target LOC, required coverage delta, or specific tests to add.)"
  — Answer (user): "1, 3, 4, 5". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "3) Constraints & delivery preferences (choose or freeform answer):
   - Are public API changes allowed? (Yes / No — default: No)
   - Deployment constraints: must be merged in one PR or prefer small incremental PRs? (One big PR / Incremental PRs — default: Incremental)
   - Any timeline, review-approver, or runtime constraints (e.g., zero-downtime, no changes to runtime config, must not alter health semantics)? If yes, please state briefly."
  — Answer (user): "changes allowed, incremental, no timeline". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

(End Appendix)
data.title: Refactor proxy server

data.description:
Headline
--------
Return HTTP 429 (text/plain) when llama-server reports zero available slots (no Retry-After). Add unit and integration tests and update proxy documentation to describe client retry guidance.

## Problem statement

When a POST to /v1/chat/completions is forwarded to a local llama-server that reports 0 available slots (but total_slots > 0), the proxy currently logs "No available slots (...) rejecting request" and raises an HTTP 503. The response body is inconsistent/empty which leaves clients unable to decide how and when to retry.

Example log evidence:
```
2026-05-28 23:33:00,145 - WARNING - No available slots (1 total), rejecting request
INFO: 192.168.0.229:51378 - POST /v1/chat/completions HTTP/1.1 503 Service Unavailable
```

## Users

- Client applications (internal services and third-party integrations) calling the proxy's OpenAI-compatible endpoints (e.g., /v1/chat/completions).
- Service operators and SREs who need clear, machine-parseable signals for retries and alerting.
- Developers and QA who will implement and validate the behavior change.

User stories
- As a client, I want a clear and standard HTTP status when the model is busy so I can apply client-side retry logic (e.g., backoff) rather than relying on an empty 503.
- As an operator, I want the proxy to document the expected client behavior so integration tests and runbooks can be updated.

## Acceptance Criteria (Success Criteria)

1. Slot-busy response: When the proxy detects available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0 for a chat/completions request, it must return HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) with Content-Type: text/plain and a short body containing the text `"Model server busy: 0/<total_slots> slots available. Retry later."` (the numeric total_slots must be present). The response MUST NOT include a `Retry-After` header for this case.

2. Unit tests: Add unit tests under `proxy/tests/` that mock the llama-server slots endpoint returning 0 available slots and assert that the proxy returns status 429, Content-Type text/plain, the expected short body text (matching the pattern above), and that the `Retry-After` header is absent.

3. Integration test: Add or update an integration test that exercises `POST /v1/chat/completions` with a stubbed slots endpoint (or patched client) to simulate `available_slots == 0` and asserts a 429 response as above.

4. Documentation: Update `proxy/README.md` (and any other proxied docs or runbooks) to document the new slot-exhaustion semantics: slot-busy -> 429 text/plain (no Retry-After); model-loading -> 503 JSON with retry_after (unchanged). Include example curl requests/responses and guidance for client retry/backoff behavior.

5. Tests & CI: The change must include the new/updated tests and the full project test suite must pass locally. (This will be validated by running the project's test suite.)

Note: Acceptance criteria are measurable and testable; if any team wants a different plain-text message or a Retry-After header, open the Appendix question for clarifying preferences.

## Constraints

- Do not change the existing model-loading 503 JSON + `Retry-After` behavior (implemented via `_model_loading_response`) — that path must remain unchanged.
- Keep observability signals intact: ensure any existing backend signal counters (e.g., `backend_signals.concurrency_rejects`) remain incremented for the slot rejection case.
- Keep the change minimal and localised to the slot-check branch so unrelated error paths are not affected.
- Prefer a single, small code change to `proxy/proxy/server.py` and accompanying tests rather than a large refactor.

## Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Client compatibility — some clients may depend on 503 responses or the presence of a `Retry-After` header and could fail if they do not handle 429/text responses. Mitigation: document the change in `proxy/README.md`, announce it in release notes, and (optionally) record a follow-up work item to add a compatibility/config toggle if needed.

- Risk: Monitoring and alerts may rely on 503 responses. Mitigation: update alert rules and dashboards as part of the rollout where required.

- Assumption: The model-loading path (where the proxy returns JSON 503 + `retry_after`) is a separate code path and must remain unchanged.

- Scope-control: To avoid scope creep, any requested additional features (e.g., adding structured JSON or a configuration flag) should be recorded as separate child work items linked to this item rather than expanding the current scope.

## Existing state

- File: `proxy/proxy/server.py` contains the slot availability check for `v1/chat/completions`. When `available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0` the proxy currently logs a warning and raises `HTTPException(status_code=503, detail=...)` (which results in a non-uniform response body depending on the framework defaults).
- There is an existing helper `_model_loading_response(...)` that returns a JSON 503 payload with `retry_after` and `Retry-After` header; README documents that behaviour.
- Unit and integration tests related to backend resilience and slot handling live under `proxy/tests/`.

## Desired change (implementation notes)

- Code: In `proxy/proxy/server.py` replace the slot-exhausted branch that currently raises `HTTPException(status_code=503, ...)` with a plain-text response, for example:

```py
from fastapi.responses import PlainTextResponse

# when available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0:
return PlainTextResponse(
    status_code=429,
    content=f"Model server busy: 0/{total_slots} slots available. Retry later.",
    media_type="text/plain",
)
```

- Tests: Add unit tests that patch the call to the llama-server `/slots` endpoint (or patch `_http_client`) to return 0 available slots and assert the 429/text/plain response and absence of `Retry-After` header. Add an integration test that does the same against a running proxy instance (or test harness) if available.

- Docs: Update `proxy/README.md` to document the slot-busy 429 semantics, including short examples and a note that model-loading remains 503 + JSON + `retry_after`.

- Metrics/observability: Ensure the existing counter increment for concurrency rejects is preserved for the new response.

## Related work (manual seeds)

- LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 — "Mitigate llama-server ROCm crash path and add backend recovery safeguards" (completed): documents existing concurrency gates, slot checks, and model-loading 503/Retry-After behavior in `proxy/proxy/server.py` and `proxy/config.yaml`. Relevant for context and regression testing.

- LP-0MPN836LQ002YQCY — "Investigate llama-server backend connection errors causing proxy retries and concurrency spike" (completed): investigation of backend errors, retries and concurrency spikes; may contain useful test cases and historical context for concurrency rejections.

- LP-0MPHM16H9002O54V — "Add proxy timeouts and concurrency limits" (completed): contains rationale and tests around concurrency limits and slot checks.

- proxy/README.md — documents model-loading 503 + `retry_after` JSON response and general proxy behavior (update required).

## Related work (automated report)

- LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 — Mitigate llama-server ROCm crash path and add backend recovery safeguards (completed). Why related: documents the proxy's concurrency gates and slot-check logic; this work previously hardened retry/backoff and observability on backend failure paths and should be consulted for tests and runbook wording.

- LP-0MPN836LQ002YQCY — Investigate llama-server backend connection errors causing proxy retries and concurrency spike (completed). Why related: incident investigation shows how backend faults lead to retry storms and slot exhaustion; includes log signatures and test cases useful for regression coverage.

- LP-0MPHM16H9002O54V — Add proxy timeouts and concurrency limits (completed). Why related: contains prior test fixtures and reasoning for concurrency gating and is a useful precedent for unit/integration test structure.

- proxy/proxy/server.py (file) — Why related: contains the exact slot-check code path to be modified; implementation should be minimal and localised here.

- proxy/README.md (file) — Why related: documents current model-loading 503 + retry_after behavior and must be updated to describe the new slot-busy 429 semantics and migration guidance for clients.

(End automated report)

---

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "HTTP status choice when no slots are available (pick one)
   - A) Keep 503 Service Unavailable (consistent with model-loading behavior in README) — recommended
   - B) Use 429 Too Many Requests (semantically typical for rate/queue rejections)
   - C) Keep current behaviour (no body) — not recommended" — Answer (user): "B) Use 429 Too Many Requests". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Retry guidance and payload shape (pick one)
   - A) Return JSON + Retry-After header using the existing model-loading pattern...
   - B) Same JSON + header but add a new config key server.slot_retry_after...
   - C) Return JSON only (no Retry-After header).
   - D) Only use HTTP 429 with a short textual body (no structured JSON or header)." — Answer (user): "D) Only use HTTP 429 with a short textual body (no structured JSON or header)." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Tests & docs scope (pick one)
   - A) Implement code change in proxy/proxy/server.py, add unit tests under proxy/tests/, and update proxy/README.md to document the new 429 JSON + Retry behavior. (Recommended.)
   - B) Prepare a design/RFC and PR for review before implementing code.
   - C) Only update docs and tests now; code change will be done later by another engineer." — Answer (user): "A) Implement code change in proxy/proxy/server.py, add unit tests under proxy/tests/, and update proxy/README.md." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Research performed by the agent: Searched repository for "slots", "503", "Retry-After", and relevant tests. Found the slot-check code in `proxy/proxy/server.py` (slot rejection branch) and an existing `_model_loading_response` helper that returns JSON 503 + Retry-After. Worklog search returned related items LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 and LP-0MPN836LQ002YQCY which were inspected and noted above.
data.title: Return informative 503 with Retry-After and machine-readable body when no slots available

data.description:
Summary
-------
Make the LLama Proxy reachable over Tailscale at 100.79.231.101 and rename the operator-facing provider label from "Local Proxy" to "Proxy"; implement changes in-repo with tests and updated docs.

Intake draft: Make Proxy use Tailscale (LP-0MPU80LK20020L4X)

Problem statement
-----------------
Update the LLama Proxy so the operator-facing provider label and configuration use the name "Proxy" (replacing any existing "Local Proxy" label) and ensure the proxy is reachable at the Tailscale IP 100.79.231.101 (preserving existing URL path semantics such as any `/v1` prefix). The change must be implemented in-repo, fully tested, and documented.

Users
-----
- Operators / SREs: need the proxy reachable over Tailscale for remote management and monitoring. Example user story: "As an operator, I can reach the proxy UI and health endpoints at the Tailscale address so I can manage models without VPNs."
- Local developers: need the repo and test suite to continue working (tests must be runnable locally). Example user story: "As a developer, I can run the test suite locally (using a local or configured proxy URL) and the tests remain stable." 
- Automation / infra: deployment and system scripts that reference the proxy host or provider name.

Acceptance Criteria (Success Criteria)
-------------------------------------
1. Configuration: proxy/config.yaml is updated so that server.host is set to 100.79.231.101 (port remains 8000 unless otherwise configured). The repo's start scripts are updated so running the proxy using the repo tooling listens on 100.79.231.101:8000 by default or respects an explicit environment variable that overrides the host.

2. Provider naming: all user-facing occurrences of the label "Local Proxy" are replaced with "Proxy" across the repository (UI strings, docs). A case-insensitive search for the exact phrase "Local Proxy" in tracked files returns zero results after the change.

3. Backwards compatibility: the proxy accepts an incoming provider identifier of either "Local Proxy" or "Proxy" (compatibility shim) for a transition period. Unit tests verify that requests labelled with either provider resolve to the same behavior.

4. Tests & validation: add/modify unit and integration tests as needed so that:
   - A new unit test verifies proxy/config.yaml loads and server.host == "100.79.231.101" when the updated config is used.
   - Integration tests that contact a running proxy read the base URL from an environment variable (PROXY_BASE_URL or LLAMA_PROXY_URL) with a default of http://localhost:8000 so developer runs are not broken.
   - Full project test suite passes locally in a fresh dev environment with changes applied.

5. Docs & operator guidance: proxy/README.md and proxy/HOST_INSTALL.md are updated to document the new provider label and the operator verification steps to confirm the proxy is reachable at http://100.79.231.101:8000 (or the equivalent scheme/port preserved from existing settings).

6. Deliverables: changes committed to a branch named `wl-LP-0MPU80LK20020L4X-make-proxy-use-tailscale` with a clear commit message referencing LP-0MPU80LK20020L4X. The PR updates docs, tests, and the code changes and demonstrates that CI and local tests pass.

Constraints
-----------
- The Tailscale address 100.79.231.101 is only routable when the caller is on the same Tailscale network; CI and developer machines may not be on that network. Tests that reach the proxy must be parameterized (env var) or skip when the target is unreachable.
- The repository currently uses 0.0.0.0 in config and in start scripts (see existing state). Start scripts and any deployment manifests (outside this repo) may also need updates — coordinate with infra teams before rollout.
- Do not change public API surface beyond provider label renaming and provider alias compatibility; preserve request/response shapes and any /v1 path prefixes.

Risks & assumptions
-------------------
- Risk: Changing the default host may break local developer workflows and tests that assume localhost; Mitigation: parameterize tests using PROXY_BASE_URL and keep default to http://localhost:8000 for local runs.
- Risk: The Tailscale IP may not be reachable from CI or some operator locations, causing integration checks to fail; Mitigation: tests and CI should use environment-driven endpoints or mocks, and the operator verification steps should be manual and documented.
- Risk: The provider label may be persisted in external infra or dashboards (outside this repo); Mitigation: include an OPEN QUESTION to confirm external manifests and coordinate changes with infra.
- Assumption: The requested label "Local Proxy" exists in consumer-facing UI or external infra; repo scan found no occurrences in tracked files (agent-inference). Final: implement compatibility alias as a safety net.
- Scope-scope mitigation: If additional feature requests arise during implementation, record them as separate WL child items rather than expanding this scope.

Existing state (evidence)
-------------------------
- proxy/config.yaml currently contains `server.host: 0.0.0.0` (dev default).
  - File: proxy/config.yaml (line: server.host: 0.0.0.0)
- The repo start script uses a hardcoded uvicorn host flag: proxy/scripts/start-proxy.sh currently runs `uvicorn … --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`.
  - File: proxy/scripts/start-proxy.sh
- I searched the repository for the phrase "Local Proxy" and found no matches, which implies the provider label may be used outside the repo or surfaced from runtime config. Tests and examples contain hard-coded references to http://localhost:8000 which will need environment-variable parameterization.
  - Example files referencing localhost: proxy/tests/* and examples/session_client.py

Desired change (implementation notes)
------------------------------------
The implementer should perform the following conservative code & doc changes inside this repository:

1) Configuration
   - Edit proxy/config.yaml:
     - Change `server.host: 0.0.0.0` to `server.host: 100.79.231.101`
     - (Optional, recommended) Add an optional `providers:` mapping to support named endpoints, e.g.:

```yaml
providers:
  Proxy:
    host: "http://100.79.231.101:8000"
    type: "local"
```

   - Rationale: the explicit providers map provides an explicit, discoverable mapping for operator use and enables the UI to list providers.

2) Start script / runtime
   - Update proxy/scripts/start-proxy.sh to read an environment override (LLAMA_PROXY_HOST or PROXY_HOST) and default to the value in config.yaml (or to 100.79.231.101 if config not available). Minimal safe change: replace hardcoded `--host 0.0.0.0` with `--host "${LLAMA_PROXY_HOST:-100.79.231.101}"` and document the env var.

3) UI / strings
   - Find and replace any occurrences of the literal string "Local Proxy" in UI templates, JS, and docs with "Proxy". If none are found (none were found during repo scan), add a one-line comment in proxy/README.md noting that the UI label change was performed and where to look if it appears in external infra.

4) Backwards-compatible provider alias
   - Add a small compatibility shim in proxy/proxy/server.py (or equivalent) to map incoming provider identifiers: treat "Local Proxy" as an alias for "Proxy". Implement as a single helper function (e.g. normalize_provider_name()) and add unit tests for it.

5) Tests
   - Replace hard-coded http://localhost:8000 usages in tests (proxy/tests/* and examples) with a helper that chooses the base URL from environment variable PROXY_BASE_URL with default http://localhost:8000.
   - Add unit test: proxy/tests/test_config_host.py verifying config loads and contains the expected host value when the updated config is used.
   - Add unit test: proxy/tests/test_provider_alias.py verifying the compatibility shim accepts both "Local Proxy" and "Proxy".

6) Docs
   - Update proxy/README.md and proxy/HOST_INSTALL.md with the new default host and operator verification example.
   - Add a short migration note describing the provider rename and the compatibility alias with recommended timeline for removing the alias.

7) CI and local verification
   - Ensure tests use environment variable PROXY_BASE_URL so CI can continue to run tests against a CI-provided or mocked proxy, and developers can run tests locally using the default (localhost) or set PROXY_BASE_URL to http://100.79.231.101:8000 if they are on Tailscale.

Related work (automated report)
-------------------------------
- proxy/config.yaml — contains server.host (0.0.0.0) and other server settings (evidence of current defaults).
- proxy/scripts/start-proxy.sh — start script uses uvicorn and hardcodes host=0.0.0.0.
- proxy/README.md — docs that reference running on localhost; must be updated.
- Host / infra work items (found in Worklog):
  - Remove systemd service files for llama-proxy (LP-0MN6JRADW0NL3E5W) — historic changes to runtime/service management; may affect where to change host in deployed units.
  - Refactor proxy server (LP-0MPQ1VMX0000GNF4) — larger refactor that may overlap with server internals; review if active work touches the same code.
  - Return informative 503 ... (LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ) — recent changes to proxy behavior and tests; use as reference for test patterns.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
----------------------------------------
- Q: "Where should we make the change?
  - A) In this repository (proxy/config.yaml, proxy UI, or other file here).
  - B) In an external infra/deployment repo or systems UI (please supply the repo/path or the deployment system).
  - C) In some operator/admin UI (not stored in repo) — I should prepare rolling instructions only.
  - D) Other: (freeform — please specify)." — Answer (user): "A". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "What exactly should be changed? (pick one or more)
  - A) Rename the UI/provider label: “Local Proxy” → “Proxy” (cosmetic/UI text).
  - B) Change the provider key/type used by code/config from “Local Proxy” to “Proxy” (affects routing/config keys).
  - C) Change the host URL to 100.79.231.101 (please confirm scheme and port, e.g., http://100.79.231.101:8000).
  - D) All of the above.
  - E) Other (freeform)." — Answer (user): "D, url is whatever the current format is (e.g. if there is a v1 or similar in the current setting, keep it)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Scope, verification & rollout expectations
  - A) Make code/config changes in this repo, add/update tests and docs, and require full test-suite pass before merge.
  - B) Make a local/dev-only change (no CI/deploy), document the manual steps.
  - C) Ops/infra must apply the change; provide an operator playbook and verification checklist (I will prepare docs and a small test plan).
  - D) Other (freeform)." — Answer (user): "A". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Research & short findings (agent-inference / repo evidence):
- I ran a repository scan and found `server.host: 0.0.0.0` in proxy/config.yaml and a hardcoded `--host 0.0.0.0` in proxy/scripts/start-proxy.sh. I did not find the literal phrase "Local Proxy" in tracked files. Tests and examples use http://localhost:8000 in several places (proxy/tests/* and examples/session_client.py). These observations influenced the recommended changes above.

Open questions (if any)
----------------------
- OPEN QUESTION: Are there external deployment manifests (outside this repository) or orchestration templates (systemd unit files, k8s manifests, cloud-init, infra repo) that also mention the provider name or proxy host that we should update? If yes, please provide the repo/path or attach the manifest.
data.title: Make Proxy use Tailscale

data.description:
# Proxy logs: stream content as a growing string instead of JSON message stream

## Problem statement
When consumers (particularly console/terminal consumers) tail proxy logs via /logs/tail they currently receive JSON-wrapped SSE messages (e.g. {"line": "..."}). That JSON wrapper is noisy and makes simple console usage (curl, tail-style consumption) harder to read. For console output we should stream the log content itself (plain text) as it is appended, not the JSON envelope.

## Users
- Local developers and operators who use the console (curl, wget, or terminal) to tail logs.
  - User story: As an engineer running `curl -N http://localhost:8000/logs/tail?...` I want to see raw log lines as they arrive so I can read and grep them easily.
- SREs / debuggers who pipe tails into tooling scripts that expect plain text.
  - User story: As an SRE piping /logs/tail into a grep/awk pipeline, I want to receive plain-text log lines so scripts don't need to parse JSON.

## Acceptance Criteria (Success Criteria)
1. When a console-style request is made to /logs/tail (opt-in via query param `format=raw`), the SSE stream must send the initial block and subsequent events as plain text data payloads (no JSON envelope). Specifically:
   - The initial SSE `data:` event contains the last `lines` lines joined with `
` (exact same content as currently included in the `initial` JSON field), and the payload is plain text (not a JSON object).
   - Subsequent SSE `data:` events contain only the new appended text (one or more lines) as plain text. The messages must NOT include JSON object wrappers like `{ "line": "...", "source": "..." }`.
2. Default behavior without `format=raw` is unchanged (existing JSON message stream is preserved). The web UI and other JSON consumers must continue to work unchanged.
3. Unit tests are added or updated under `proxy/tests/` to validate both behaviors:
   - `test_logs_default_format` verifies the existing JSON envelope behavior remains unchanged.
   - `test_logs_raw_format` verifies that requesting `/logs/tail?format=raw` returns plain-text payloads for initial and incremental events.
4. Documentation is updated (proxy/README.md and inline docstring in `proxy/proxy/server.py`) to describe the new `format=raw` option and example usage for console clients.
5. Full project test suite passes (all tests) after changes.

> Notes: Acceptance criteria 1 and 2 are measurable by automated unit tests that assert the exact SSE payload strings.

## Constraints
- Change must be non-breaking by default; keep current JSON SSE output as the default format.
- The SSE media type remains `text/event-stream`.
- Do not modify the built-in web UI client behavior in this change (the UI should continue to use default JSON messages).
- Keep implementation minimal and localized to `/logs/tail` handler.

## Existing state
- `proxy/proxy/server.py` currently yields SSE events where the `data:` payload is JSON: initial messages look like `{"initial": "...", "source": "proxy"}` and incremental messages look like `{"line": "...", "source": "proxy"}`. The embedded web UI (client-side JS in the same file) JSON.parse()s these messages and renders `obj.line` or `obj.initial` into the panes.

## Desired change
- Add an opt-in `format=raw` query parameter to `/logs/tail`. When `format=raw` is present, the server will send the initial block and subsequent appended text as plain text payloads in SSE `data:` messages (no JSON envelope). When `format` is not `raw`, preserve the current JSON behavior.
- Add unit tests exercising both formats.
- Update proxy/README.md and the function docstring for `/logs/tail` to document `format=raw` and give a console usage example (`curl -N 'http://localhost:8000/logs/tail?lines=200&format=raw'`).

## Related work
- LP-0MN7LEGVX003YTSL — Improve log output (parent) — broader work to improve logs.
- LP-0MNWAC6PM007BFWQ — Split logging web UI: proxy + llama-server tail — related UI work (do NOT change UI in this item).
- proxy/proxy/server.py — current implementation of `/logs/tail` and embedded web UI (see function `tail_logs`).
- proxy/tests/test_logs.py — existing tests around log path resolution; add extra tests here.

---

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- Q: "Consumers / scope — which consumers must be supported by this change?
  A) Only the built-in proxy web UI (proxy/proxy/server.py) — update server + UI and tests accordingly.
  B) The web UI plus other internal automation/tests that may parse /logs/tail — ensure any tests/tooling are updated too.
  C) All consumers and backward compatibility must be preserved (see Q3 for how).
  Suggested: B (web UI + update any tests found to depend on format)." — Answer (user): "You are over thinking this. This only applies to the console output. Instead of the entire JSON string being ooutput simply show the content as it is streamed." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Exact semantics of “growing string” — which of these matches your intent?
  A) Initial SSE message contains the last-N lines; subsequent SSE events provide raw appended text chunks (plain string in data, not JSON). Client will append e.data to a single text area (most efficient for UI append).
  B) SSE events send the entire log content each time as one string (replace the full text on each update) — simpler but inefficient for large logs.
  C) Keep JSON but change the schema to a single content field, e.g. {\"content\": \"<full-or-incremental>\"} — less breaking for JSON-based clients.
  Suggested: A (initial block then incremental raw text chunks)." — Answer (user): "You are over thinking this. This only applies to the console output. Instead of the entire JSON string being ooutput simply show the content as it is streamed." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Backwards compatibility & work split — how to deploy?
  A) Non-breaking: add a query parameter (e.g., /logs/tail?format=string) and keep the current JSON default; update the web UI to request format=string (recommended safe approach).
  B) Breaking: change the default SSE format to the new growing-string behavior and update UI/tests to match (simpler but will break existing JSON consumers).
  C) Add the format param but do NOT update the UI or tests in this work item (leave UI/tests as follow-up tasks).
  Suggested: A (add format param, update UI/tests in same work item)." — Answer (user): "You are over thinking this. This only applies to the console output. Instead of the entire JSON string being ooutput simply show the content as it is streamed." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Notes on interpretation and assumptions made (kept minimal):
- The user explicitly restricted scope to console output. To avoid breaking existing JSON consumers (web UI, tests), this intake proposes an opt-in query parameter `format=raw`. If you prefer a different activation method (header-based detection or a different param name), please say so and I'll adjust the intake.



## Related work (automated report)
- LP-0MN7LEGVX003YTSL — Improve log output (parent): parent item that covers broader logging improvements; this child should remain aligned with its acceptance criteria and scheduling.
- LP-0MNWAC6PM007BFWQ — Split logging web UI: proxy + llama-server tail: documents prior work to separate proxy and llama-server log tails for the UI; this intake intentionally avoids UI changes to remain non-breaking.
- LP-0MNWBTDZ3002MCTQ — Merged Logs SSE (opt): earlier exploration of SSE merging and alternate payload shapes; review for ideas/conflicts before implementation.
- proxy/proxy/server.py (function `tail_logs`): primary implementation point — currently emits JSON-wrapped SSE messages and is the code to modify for `format=raw` behavior.
- proxy/tests/test_logs.py: existing tests around log path resolution; a new test file or additional tests should be added here to validate `format=raw` behavior.


## Risks & assumptions
- Risk: If the default SSE payload format is changed for all clients, the web UI and other JSON consumers may break. Mitigation: this intake proposes an opt-in `format=raw` so default behavior is unchanged.
- Risk: Tests or automation that parse /logs/tail as JSON may regress. Mitigation: add unit tests for both formats and update any internal tooling that parses the stream.
- Risk: Log rotation or very long initial payloads may cause large single-event bodies in `format=raw`. Mitigation: keep the current `lines` parameter semantics and consider bounding large initial payloads.
- Assumption: "console output" means casual terminal consumers (curl/EventSource consumers that do not JSON-parse event payloads). If this assumption is incorrect, please clarify.
- Scope-scope mitigation: any additional feature requests or UX changes discovered during implementation will be captured as separate WL child items rather than expanding scope here.
data.title: Proxy logs: stream content as a growing string instead of JSON message stream

data.description:
# Proxy: show only streamed 'content' on console (suppress full JSON chunk logs)

Problem statement

The proxy currently prints full streaming chunk JSON objects to the console (example: `STREAM CHUNK | data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":" 👋"}}], ...}`), which is noisy for interactive use. We should change the console output so it shows only the streamed text content (the `delta.content` payloads) as they arrive, while leaving file logs and any other external log consumers unchanged. If parsing fails, the original chunk should be logged to the file console fallback so no information is lost.

Users

- Operators and developers running the proxy locally who watch console output during development, testing, or troubleshooting.
- On-call engineers who may tail the service stdout for quick feedback during incidents.

User stories

- As a developer, I want the console to show only the text being streamed so the terminal is readable during live sessions.
- As an operator, I want to avoid noisy JSON envelopes in console logs so I can quickly see model output without scanning JSON.

Acceptance Criteria (measurable)

1. Console-only content: When a streaming response chunk is received and contains JSON with `choices[].delta.content`, the console handler prints only the concatenation of those `delta.content` strings in order, without the surrounding JSON envelope. Verify by running a sample streaming request that produces SSE chunks containing `delta.content` and observing the console (example input -> expected console output). (Verification: automated unit test that captures console StreamHandler output and asserts equality.)

2. No added newlines: The console must not add newline characters; a newline is only printed if and when the streamed `delta.content` includes a newline character itself. (Verification: unit test uses chunks with and without `
` and asserts console output behavior.)

3. File logs unchanged: File/rotating log handler(s) must continue to record the original full JSON chunk strings unchanged. (Verification: after sending a streaming request, inspect the file log and confirm the `STREAM CHUNK | data: {...}` line is present in the file logs unchanged.)

4. Fallback behavior: If parsing the chunk as streaming JSON fails or if the chunk is not in the expected form, the console should fall back to printing the original chunk text (to avoid data loss). (Verification: unit test forces a malformed or non-JSON chunk and asserts console shows original chunk.)

5. Tests & docs: Add unit tests covering console parsing behavior and add documentation notes to `proxy/README.md` (logging configuration/behaviour). Also: "All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries." (Verification: PR includes tests and README updates.)

6. Full test suite: Full project test suite must pass with the new changes. (Verification: local/full CI run passes.)

Constraints

- Do not change file handlers, file log rotation, or any existing log consumers; only the console output (console StreamHandler) may be modified.
- Default behaviour: content-only console output will be applied by default (no runtime toggle required) per stakeholder decision.
- Changes must be conservative and include a safe fallback path when parsing fails.
- Avoid adding significant CPU/latency overhead to the request/stream path; parsing should be limited to the first ~2KB of the chunk when practical.

Existing state

- The proxy logs streaming chunks via `log_response_chunk(chunk: bytes)` in `proxy/proxy/server.py`, which currently decodes the bytes and logs the full chunk string with `logger.info(f"STREAM CHUNK | {chunk_str}")`.
- `setup_logging(config)` in `proxy/proxy/server.py` configures two handlers: a TimedRotatingFileHandler (file logs) and a StreamHandler (console). By default both handlers receive the same log records.

Desired change (implementation notes)

- Option A (recommended): Implement a custom console handler or formatter that, for records beginning with the `STREAM CHUNK |` prefix, attempts to extract the streamed text content and render only that to the console stream. The file handler's formatter must remain unchanged so file logs continue to include the full JSON chunk. Implementation hints:
  - Add a helper `extract_streamed_content_from_chunk(chunk_str: str) -> str | None` that:
    - Looks for SSE `data: ` payloads in the chunk and extracts JSON objects if present.
    - For each parsed JSON payload, collects `choices[].delta.content` tokens (in order) and concatenates them into the returned string.
    - If no JSON `data:` sections are found, attempt to parse the whole chunk as JSON and extract `delta.content` similarly.
    - On any parse error return `None` so the console fallback uses the original chunk text.
  - Implement `ConsoleStreamHandler(logging.StreamHandler)` overriding `emit()` to: detect `STREAM CHUNK | ` prefix; call `extract_streamed_content_from_chunk`; if it returns a non-empty string, write it to the stream using `self.stream.write()` without appending an extra newline; only write newline(s) that are present in the content itself. For non-STREAM CHUNK records call the base `emit()` to preserve behaviour.
  - Keep the file handler attached to the same logger with existing formatter so file logs remain unchanged.
  - Keep parsing windows bounded (e.g., inspect only the first N bytes/characters of chunk) to avoid unbounded CPU work.

Related work

- proxy/proxy/server.py — streaming handlers, `log_response_chunk`, `setup_logging` (primary implementation location). (file)
- proxy/README.md — logging configuration and documentation (update README with behaviour change). (file)
- proxy/config.yaml — existing logging configuration; although stakeholder selected default behaviour, note any relevant config locations. (file)
- LP-0MN7LEGVX003YTSL — "Improve log output" (in-review) — related discussion and prior work on log UI and severity colouring (work item)
- LP-0MNWBTEBR000EJPS — "Log File Resilience" (completed) — ensures file handlers/rotation behaviour (work item)

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Target confirmation: is the intended change for the proxy implementation in repository path proxy/proxy/server.py (log_response_chunk + setup_logging)?" — Answer (user): "A". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Behaviour for streaming events: when a streamed SSE chunk contains JSON with delta.content, which should the console show?" — Answer (user): "A except that a new line should only be displayed when one is sent in the delta". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Configuration: should this be configurable or applied by default?" — Answer (user): "B" (Make content-only console output the default with no config). Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Notes / quick code references

- Evidence: `proxy/proxy/server.py` contains `log_response_chunk(chunk: bytes)` and `logger.info(f"STREAM CHUNK | {chunk_str}")` (current behaviour). See repository for exact line references.
- Evidence: `setup_logging(config)` configures a `TimedRotatingFileHandler` (file) and a `StreamHandler` (console) — the console handler is the target for change.

Open questions

- OPEN QUESTION: Are there any external consumers that parse the console (stdout) JSON stream that must be preserved (for example, systemd/journal parsers, container log collectors)? The user indicated the change should "not change any consumers of logs" which we interpret to mean file handlers and external collectors that read the file logs; confirm whether altering stdout is acceptable for running deployments that expect the old JSON shape. (Directed to: user/operators).
data.title: Proxy: show only streamed 'content' on console (suppress full JSON chunk logs)

data.description:
# Prompt resolution tests

Unit tests for the prompt resolver verifying local override vs repo default precedence, alias exact vs wildcard precedence, size and encoding handling, and config validation for missing mode.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Unit tests added at `proxy/tests/test_prompt_resolution.py`.
- Tests cover:
  - Local override (`.sorraAgents/prompts/<alias>.txt`) wins when present.
  - Repo default (`proxy/prompts/<file>`) used when no override present.
  - Exact alias matches take precedence over wildcard matches (uses `get_model_config()` semantics).
  - Oversized files (>64KB) are ignored (resolver returns `None`) and a warning is logged; requests still forward (no crash).
  - Non-UTF-8 files are safely skipped and logged.
  - If a model entry includes `system_prompt` but omits a valid `mode`, config validation fails with a clear error.
- All tests pass locally and in CI.

## Minimal Implementation

- Add pytest-based tests using `tmp_path`/`monkeypatch` to simulate files and model config.
- Test the resolver function directly (e.g. `proxy.prompt_resolver.resolve_system_prompt`).

## Files

- `proxy/tests/test_prompt_resolution.py` (new)

## Related

Reuses `get_model_config()` behaviour in `proxy/proxy/server.py` for alias precedence.
data.title: Prompt resolution tests

data.description:
# Prompt composition tests

Unit tests that validate message composition logic: `override` replaces system messages; `prepend` inserts file content as the first system message.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Tests added at `proxy/tests/test_prompt_composition.py`.
- For `override` mode: forwarded payload system messages are replaced by file content.
- For `prepend` mode: forwarded payload system messages begin with file content followed by client-supplied system messages.
- Tests exercise both streaming and non-streaming code paths if applicable (mocking SSE vs non-SSE payloads).
- Tests pass locally and in CI.

## Minimal Implementation

- Mock the backend (e.g., using respx or httpx.MockTransport) to capture outgoing request body and assert message order/contents.
- Use test client to build outgoing payloads and assert transforms.

## Files

## Related

- Message composition logic in `proxy/proxy/server.py`.

data.description:
# Prompt resolver

Implement `resolve_system_prompt(alias_or_model, model_cfg)` that finds and returns prompt file content (no caching), enforcing UTF-8 and max size (64KB). Searches local override then repo defaults.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implementation located in `proxy/prompt_resolver.py` (or equivalent) with unit tests covering behavior (see `Prompt resolution tests`).
- Search precedence:
  1) Project-local override: `.sorraAgents/prompts/<alias>.txt`
  2) Repo default path specified in `model_cfg.system_prompt.file` (relative to repo root) or `proxy/prompts/<file>` when given as filename
  3) Return `None` if not found or invalid
- Enforce size limit (64KB). Oversize files are ignored and logged.
- Non-UTF-8 files are ignored and logged.
- No caching: file is read on every resolve call.

## Minimal Implementation

- Small module `proxy/prompt_resolver.py` with `resolve_system_prompt(alias, model_cfg)`.
- Read files using `Path(...).read_bytes()` then decode with utf-8 and size check.

## Files

- `proxy/prompt_resolver.py` (new)

## Dependencies

- Depends on: Prompt resolution tests (LP-0MPXTYWL70047KKD).
data.title: Prompt resolver

data.description:
# Message injection

Hook the resolver into the request handling path to apply the resolved prompt per `mode` (override|prepend) before forwarding to backend.

## Acceptance Criteria

- When a request targets an alias with `system_prompt` configured:
  - `mode == override` → outgoing payload system messages are replaced by file content.
  - `mode == prepend` → file content is inserted as the first system message before any client-supplied system messages.
- Composition applied consistently for streaming and non-streaming requests.
- Preserve existing alias precedence and wildcard semantics via `get_model_config()`.
- Covered by unit and integration tests (Prompt composition tests and Prompt injection integration test).

## Minimal Implementation

- Update outgoing payload composition in `proxy/proxy/server.py` (the code path that prepares messages to send to backend) to call the resolver and transform `body_json["messages"]` accordingly.
- Add minimal logging when prompts are applied or skipped (do not log prompt contents in full by default).

## Files

- Changes to `proxy/proxy/server.py`.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: Prompt composition tests (LP-0MPXTZ0BQ0010ESI) and Prompt injection integration test (LP-0MPXTZ3HM00049F9).
data.title: Message injection

data.description:
# Prompt debug endpoint

Add a gated debug endpoint to inspect resolved prompt and source path for QA (enabled only when server.debug true or bound to localhost).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Endpoint (e.g., `GET /debug/prompt?alias=<alias>`) returns sanitized JSON: `{ alias, resolved: boolean, mode, source_path (or null), content_preview, size_bytes }`.
- Endpoint is gated by `server.debug` config flag or allowed only on localhost (not exposed in production by default).
- Endpoint does not return full prompt content by default; returns a short preview (first 200 chars) unless explicit `full=true` and debug mode enabled.
- Covered by unit tests.

## Minimal Implementation

- Add FastAPI route in `proxy/proxy/server.py` that calls `resolve_system_prompt()` and returns sanitized output.
- Guard route using `config.get('server', {}).get('debug', False)` or `request.client.host == '127.0.0.1'`.

## Files

- Changes to `proxy/proxy/server.py`.

## Dependencies

- Depends on Message injection (LP-0MPXTZE450024RVI) and Prompt composition tests (LP-0MPXTZ0BQ0010ESI).

Short summary

Verify proxyctl resolves start script, pidfile and log paths from env and config with correct fallbacks.

## Acceptance Criteria

- When LLAMA_START_SCRIPT env var is set, proxyctl uses it to start the proxy.
- When proxy/config.yaml contains server.llama_start_script, proxyctl uses that path if LLAMA_START_SCRIPT is not set.
- With no overrides, proxyctl resolves the repository default proxy/scripts/start-proxy.sh path.
- Default pidfile path resolves to "$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/llama-proxy/proxy.pid" when XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is set; fallback to XDG_STATE_HOME-based path if not.
- Logging directory is detected from proxy/config.yaml logging.directory with a fallback to a repo-local logs directory when permissions prevent writing to the configured location.

## Minimal Implementation

- Add automated integration tests that run proxyctl in a temporary environment where LLAMA_START_SCRIPT, XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, and config.yaml are varied and assert outputs or side-effects (pidfile/logfile created).
- Expose a --debug-resolve flag to print resolved paths for test assertions.

## Deliverables

- proxy/tests/test_proxyctl_config.py (pytest) or shell-based tests
- Small helper test start script

Verify proxyctl start behavior: default background detach, optional --foreground, pidfile created, and health polling succeeds within timeout.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `proxyctl start` (background) launches the configured start script, writes the pidfile, and exits 0 after /health returns 200 within server.llama_startup_timeout.
- `proxyctl start --foreground` runs in foreground and streams output; exit code 0 if the child process exits cleanly, non-zero on failure.
- If the start script is missing or fails immediately, proxyctl exits non-zero and leaves logs for diagnosis.

## Minimal Implementation

- Integration test uses a fake start script that launches a simple HTTP server responding on /health; assert pidfile created and health polled.

- test helper start script

Short summary

Verify proxyctl stop behavior: SIGTERM, graceful wait, SIGKILL fallback, pidfile cleanup.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `proxyctl stop` sends SIGTERM to PID from pidfile and waits up to 10s for exit; returns 0 when process exits.
- If process ignores SIGTERM, proxyctl escalates to SIGKILL and returns a non-zero exit code indicating forced kill.
- If no pidfile or process not running, `proxyctl stop` returns 0 and prints "proxy not running".

## Minimal Implementation

- Integration tests start mock processes (one that exits on TERM, one that ignores TERM) and verify exit behavior and pidfile removal.

## Deliverables

- proxy/tests/test_proxyctl_stop.py

data.description:
Headline

Increase Qwen context window to 128k (131,072) to support longer prompts and reduce prompt-splitting for users relying on long-context interactions.

Problem statement

Qwen3 is being used as a local model backend but there are inconsistencies in the repository about its configured context window. The goal is to standardize and increase the Qwen context window to 128k (131,072) where appropriate so longer conversations and large single-turn prompts work reliably.

Users

- Developers and operators running the local proxy and llama-server: need predictable model startup and resource requirements.
- QA engineers / Integrators: need to validate that long-context prompts succeed end-to-end.
- End users (interactive systems, long-document tasks): benefit from increased context capacity.

Example user stories

- As a developer, I want the Qwen model to accept 128k context so I can run long prompts without truncation.
- As QA, I want a reproducible smoke test that verifies the model starts with the expected ctx-size and can process a large prompt.

Acceptance Criteria (Success Criteria)

1. Configuration: All authoritative configuration locations for the Qwen model (at minimum: `models.ini` Qwen3 entry and the `qwen3` case in `start-llama.sh`) are updated or verified to use `131072` (128k) as the context size, with a short code diff that demonstrates the change.

2. Documentation: `proxy/README.md` and any other user-facing docs that mention Qwen context limits are updated to document the 128k context window and any resource implications (RAM/CPU notes and recommended host sizing). (Measurable: docs files changed in the PR and listed in the PR description.)

3. Smoke test: A manual smoke test is documented and executed: start the proxy/llama-server with the Qwen model, confirm startup logs or process args show `--ctx-size 131072` (or equivalent), and run a large prompt (example provided) that completes successfully without context-truncation. Test steps and the result must be attached to the work item as a comment.

4. Test suite: The full project test suite (unit/integration/playwright where applicable) runs and passes after the change. (Measurable: CI/locally run test results attached to the work item.)

5. Traceability: Changes are delivered in a single PR that references this work item (LP-0MPY3HSXD002ZPJL), includes the verification steps, and updates documentation. (Measurable: PR linked in work item.)

Constraints

- Memory/resource impact: increasing context window increases memory usage for the llama-server process; target hosts must have sufficient RAM to support Qwen with 128k context. Document expected RAM implications in the PR.
- Router vs single-model start differences: the repo contains both a `models.ini` preset (used by router mode) and a `start-llama.sh` single-model startup script; both must be considered when changing canonical settings.
- Backwards compatibility: ensure the change does not break machines configured for smaller contexts; provide rollback instructions.

Existing state

- Work item description: "Qwen currenly has a 66k context window but the model is capable of more. Lets try increasing it to 128k." (source: LP-0MPY3HSXD002ZPJL initial description).
- Repository evidence:
  - `models.ini` contains a section `[Qwen3]` with `ctx-size = 131072` (already 128k). (file: `models.ini`)
  - `start-llama.sh` `qwen3` case sets `CONTEXT=163840` (160k) and contains a comment saying "Max is 262144, max tried so far 131072" (file: `start-llama.sh`). This is inconsistent with `models.ini`.
  - `proxy/README.md` mentions "Context window limits" in the Sessions section and documents general limitations of llama-server's KV cache (file: `proxy/README.md`).
  - No occurrences of "66k" were found in the repository when searching code and docs; the origin of the "66k" claim is unknown and is recorded as an open question in the Appendix.

Desired change

- Align the canonical Qwen context size to 128k (131,072) across relevant configuration and startup scripts, and update docs to reflect the supported context size and any host sizing guidance. Provide a short smoke-test and verification steps in the PR description.

Related work (automated report)

- `models.ini` (path: `models.ini`) — Qwen3 entry: `ctx-size = 131072`. This appears to be the router-mode preset for Qwen and already sets 128k.
  Relevance: authoritative for router-mode; confirms 128k is already present in one config location.

- `start-llama.sh` (path: `start-llama.sh`) — single-model startup script; the `qwen3` case sets `CONTEXT=163840` (160k) and contains a comment referring to prior trials at 131072. Relevance: single-model startup differs from models.ini and should be aligned.

- `proxy/README.md` (path: `proxy/README.md`) — documents session behaviour and notes "Context window limits" for llama-server. Relevance: user-facing doc that should be updated to reflect 128k support and any operational notes.

- Work item: "Investigate high CPU usage in llama-server" (LP-0MPNTJ165004M5GX) — closed/completed; contains references to Qwen child process CPU behaviour. Relevance: historical investigation into Qwen runtime behaviour; may contain useful troubleshooting notes for this change.

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Memory pressure / out-of-memory on hosts due to larger context. Mitigation: add host sizing note and require smoke test on a suitably provisioned dev host before rollout.
- Risk: Inconsistent config locations may lead to different runtime values (router vs single-model). Mitigation: update both `models.ini` and `start-llama.sh` and add a short verification test in smoke test steps.
- Risk: Unexpected regressions in llama-server startup flags or model loading. Mitigation: conservative rollback instructions and a small canary smoke test.
- Assumptions: the Qwen model and the installed llama-server build support a 128k ctx-size on the intended hardware. If not, a follow-up task will be required.
- Scope creep mitigation: any additional feature requests discovered while working (e.g., adding automated integration tests) will be recorded as separate work items and not added to this change.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Target context size — which should we set as the canonical Qwen context size?
- A) 128k (131,072)  ← suggested / typical interpretation of \"128k\"
- B) ~160k (163,840)  ← value currently used in start-llama.sh qwen3 case
- C) 256k (262,144)
- D) Other (please specify exact integer)"
  - Answer (user): "A". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Where should the config changes be applied? (select one)
- A) Update models.ini only (router/preset)
- B) Update start-llama.sh only (single-model start)
- C) Update both models.ini and start-llama.sh (keep them consistent)  ← recommended
- D) Also update docs and README entries (I can update README entries and any relevant docs if you want)"
  - Answer (user): "D". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes. Note: the user chose to ensure docs/README are updated; the intake assumes the implementation will also verify and align both configuration locations (models.ini and start-llama.sh) as part of the change unless instructed otherwise — this is recorded as an OPEN QUESTION for implementers to confirm if they do NOT want both code locations updated.

- Q: "How should success be validated? (select one or more)
- A) Manual smoke test: start the proxy/llama-server with Qwen and verify startup logs/llama-server args include the ctx-size and a basic prompt runs
- B) Add an automated integration test / script that starts the server in the dev environment and asserts the ctx-size is applied (may require CI or local test harness)
- C) Rely on existing test suite only (run full project tests after change)
- D) Other (please specify)"
  - Answer (user): "A and C". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Research performed by the agent (files inspected): `models.ini`, `start-llama.sh`, `proxy/README.md`. Evidence: matched `ctx-size` entries in `models.ini`, the `qwen3` case in `start-llama.sh`, and README session/context notes. The repository did not reveal any occurrence of "66k" (search performed across repo).

OPEN QUESTIONS

- The original work-item description mentions a current 66k context window but the repository configs do not contain a 66k value. Where does the 66k figure come from (runtime observation, external system, or typo)? (Ask: user / operator to confirm.)
- The user selected option D for Q2 (update docs). Should the implementer still change both `models.ini` and `start-llama.sh` to align to 128k, or only update docs and leave code changes to a later implementation step? (Ask: implementer / operator)


--
Related artifacts discovered and summarized above were used to prepare this intake draft. If you want me to proceed and mark intake complete I will update LP-0MPY3HSXD002ZPJL with this draft and run the effort-and-risk estimation step.
data.title: Increase Qwen context windo

Problem

Add integration tests that assert model_switch_in_progress is true during a background model load and active_query is true while a local request is in-flight.

## Acceptance Criteria
